title: assistant-diagrams Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-diagrams skill's code-derived diagram workflow.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-diagrams skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against diagram input/output contracts, source evidence, placement, and gap handling.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Architecture diagram derived from code
cases.purpose: Checks that architecture diagrams are generated from inspected source files and returned as valid Mermaid with evidence, placement, and gaps.
cases.prompt: Create an architecture diagram for the skill eval runner using tools/evals/run-skill-evals.sh and its lib helpers.
setup_context: The assistant-diagrams skill instructions are active.
The user named specific source files and asked for an architecture diagram.
No existing diagram file path was requested.
expected_behavior: Resolves diagram_type as architecture and format as mermaid.
Reads the named runner and helper files before drawing relationships.
Outputs diagram_code as a Mermaid fenced code block.
Uses real file and function names rather than aspirational components.
Returns Evidence with source files or symbols used.
Returns Placement as inline only when no output file was requested.
Returns Gaps for any source file or relationship that could not be verified.
pass_criteria: The response includes diagram_type and diagram_code.
The response includes a Mermaid diagram.
The response cites source evidence from tools/evals files.
The response states placement and gaps.
The response does not invent components absent from source.
fail_signals: Draws from memory without reading source files.
Includes aspirational boxes or relationships not present in code.
Omits evidence paths or symbol names.
Outputs prose only with no diagram_code.

cases.title: Ambiguous diagram asks bounded clarification
cases.purpose: Checks that missing diagram_type or scope triggers a bounded clarification instead of a guessed diagram.
cases.prompt: Draw a diagram for this project.
setup_context: The assistant-diagrams skill instructions are active.
The user did not specify diagram_type, scope, or source_files.
The current project has multiple plausible diagram targets.
expected_behavior: Recognizes that required diagram_type and scope are unresolved.
Asks a concise clarification with a small set of diagram type choices.
Mentions scope or source_files needed to avoid guessing.
Does not generate a diagram before input contract fields are resolved.
Keeps the response bounded rather than asking broad open-ended questions.
pass_criteria: The response asks for diagram_type and scope or equivalent choices.
The response offers supported diagram types such as architecture, sequence, flow, or ER.
The response does not output diagram_code yet.
The response explains that source files or scope drive accuracy.
fail_signals: Generates a diagram without resolving type or scope.
Guesses the project architecture from memory.
Asks an unbounded generic question with no options.
